channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: ASCE Student Chapter Meeting
item.description: Date: Thursday, April 2, 2026 6:00 PM - 7:00 PM - Join your friends for an evening of relaxation and games. Play some of ASCE’s favorites, or bring your own. Pizza will be provided.
item.author: Students

item.title: Financial Research Resources Available to All RPI Students
item.description: All RPI students have access to financial research tools through the Lally School of Management, including Bloomberg, FactSet, S&P Capital IQ, Wharton Research Data Services (WRDS), and more. Visit the Lally website to learn more about available resources and how to access them.
item.author: Students

item.title: Success Center Tutoring for Chemistry I, II, and Organic
item.description: Did you know the Success Center offers small group tutoring in Chemistry I, II and Organic Chemistry SEVEN days a week! Check out our small group tutoring schedule and sign up today with our amazing peer tutors: https://rpi.mywconline.com/.
item.author: Students

item.title: Success Center Tabling at the Union Every Wednesday
item.description: Meet your tutors every Wednesday, 12–2 PM in the Union. Stop by the Student Success Center table to meet our tutors, grab a Puck Cookie, and book a session on the spot. We offer Drop-In Tutoring at three locations (Success Center, ECAV, and Nason), Small Group Tutoring for upper-level courses, and One-to-One Tutoring, new this semester. No appointment needed to say hi. Come find us!
item.author: Students

item.title: Register for Commencement!
item.description: In the Tassel platform, register for commencement. Confirm attendance, approve your name recording, and claim tickets. Registration closes on Sunday, April 19th, 2026. If you have any questions, reach out to commencement@rpi.edu
item.author: Students

item.title: Summer 2026 Meal Plan Announcement
item.description: For Summer 2026, only Block Plans will be offered. These plans are available to students living on or off campus during the summer semester. Students who will be back living on campus for Fall 2026 are required to enroll in a residential meal plan for that semester. Additionally, Student and Community RAD may be used at all campus dining and retail locations. For more information please click the link above or email auxiliaryservices@rpi.edu with any questions.
item.author: Community

item.title: Department of Economics Spring Seminar Series: Edson Severnini, Boston College
item.description: Please join us as the Department of Economics welcomes Dr. Edson Severnini, who will present: “The Social Lifecycle Impacts of Power Plant Siting in the Historical United States” next Wednesday, April 1st at 3:30 PM in SAGE 5510.
item.author: Community

item.title: The O.T. Sawson Multidisciplinary Design Lab announces New Director!
item.description: We are thrilled to announce the appointment of Professor Sandipan Mishra as the new Director of the O.T. Swanson Multidisciplinary Design Laboratory (The Design Lab), effective March 1, 2026. A distinguished faculty member in the Department of Mechanical, Aerospace, and Nuclear Engineering, Professor Mishra steps into this leadership role at a pivotal moment for engineering design at RPI.
item.author: Community

item.title: Information Security - Focus on Phishing Prevention Course
item.description: Date: Thursday, April 2nd, 2026 2:00 PM - 2:30 PM - Location: LOW Building 3116 - Please join us for our upcoming 30-minute course for Cybersecurity Awareness - Focus on Phishing Prevention. There will be snacks and beverages provided during the course.
item.author: Community

item.title: Meet the RPI Engineers Guiding Artemis II to the Moon and Back
item.description: RPI News spoke with Maeve Marshall and Paul McKee, two recent graduates working in the control room of Artemis II. “The Flight Operations Directorate ‘drives’ the spacecraft during the mission,” McKee says, “but they look to the nerds in the back room — me and my friends — if something starts to act funny.”
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI Chief of Public Safety Patricia Groeber: A Trailblazer for Women in Law Enforcement
item.description: Patricia Groeber, Chief of Public Safety at RPI, was recently featured in “Women in Law Enforcement: Trailblazing Troopers of the New York State Police,” a video highlighting women who helped reshape the profession, including some of the first to serve in the New York State Police (NYSP). In the video, Groeber reflects on making history as the first Deputy Superintendent of the NYSP and the challenges she overcame being among the early female members of the force.
item.author: Community

item.title: Lally Launches New Center for Management of Ethics in Emerging Technology
item.description: Lally is proud to announce the launch of the Center for Management of Ethics in Emerging Technologies. The Center will integrate ethics into education, support student and faculty research, host seminars, and provide thought leadership on ethics in emerging technologies. Future plans include white papers, faculty grants, and visiting experts to advance responsible leadership.
item.author: Community

item.title: 11th Annual Graduate Research Symposium
item.description: Date: Friday, April 3, 2026 1:00 PM - 6:00 PM - Register to attend the 11th Annual Graduate Research Symposium to learn about the great research our graduate students are doing here at RPI. Events include podium presentation talks (1:00 - 2:30 p.m.), the final round of the Three Minute Thesis competition (3:00 - 4:15 p.m.), and the poster presentation session and networking reception (4:30 - 6:00 p.m.).
item.author: Community

item.title: RPI Community Easter Dinner
item.description: Date: Sunday, April 5, 2026 6:30 PM - 9:30 PM - This Dinner is a yearly event hosted by Rensselaer Christian Association, Cru RPI, and Rensselaer Newman to serve the RPI community. We invite all of you to come enjoy a good meal and fellowship. If you are planning to attend, we ask that you RSVP using the form below. This will allow us to make sure we have enough food for everyone. https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSc-4w8TCGuyrUUZrIVWBXnG0Ws7ScX8fNpxbIryK5BSxkr5qw/viewform
item.author: Community

item.title: Join us for RPI’s Watch Party for NASA’s Artemis II Launch
item.description: Wednesday, April 1, 2026 5:45 PM - 11:00 PM - We warmly invite you to join RPI’s Watch Party for NASA’s Artemis II Launch. The event is scheduled for April 1, 2026, starting at 5:45 PM ET in LOW 3051 (please note that the launch time is subject to change by NASA). We will begin with a few exciting short talks/presentations along with snacks and beverages, and there will also be plenty of fun giveaways/goodies.
item.author: Community

item.title: Rensselaer Faculty and Staff In Person Workshop - Reviewing Supported Whiteboarding Tools On Campus for Courses or Meetings
item.description:
Date: Wednesday, April 15, 2026 12:00 PM - 1:00 PM - In this workshop we will take a look at two DotCIO supported whiteboarding tools available on campus. The first, is working with the whiteboard feature in Webex. The second tool is in Box, called Canvas. This session will offer an overview on these two similar tools giving you an opportunity to learn a little more about them and potentially consider using them in your classroom or meeting space. Please register for this session by 4/10 using…

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
